summary: SBE downloader patch landed in wrong module — never invoked for format=html sources (regression of 5a794b5e)
description:
## Discovery

Surfaced 2026-06-04 during the dry-run of 79801268 (SBE Zoroastrian re-acquire).
The dry-run was supposed to show chunk-shift diffs between legacy and re-acquired
Yasna raws. Instead it showed **byte-identical** output: legacy `yasna-30.txt` was
24,575 bytes; re-acquired `yasna-30.txt` was also 24,575 bytes (sha256 match).

Direct test of `sacred_texts.extract_text_page()` on the live sbe31008.htm HTML
proves the patch DOES work when invoked: 42,004-char input → 11,498-char output,
all `Footnotes` apparatus + `<FONT SIZE="1">` inline refs stripped.

The patch just isn't being called.

## Root cause

`acquire.load_downloader(format, url)` resolves the downloader module via:

    scripts/acquire.py:33-55

    FORMAT_DISPATCH: dict[str, str] = {
        "html":       "generic_html",   # ← all html sacred-texts URLs land here
        "html_multi": "sacred_texts",   # ← only html_multi reaches the patched code
        "sefaria_api": "sefaria",
        "access_to_insight": "access_to_insight",
    }
    DOMAIN_DISPATCH: dict[str, str] = {
        "gnosis.org": "gnosis_org",
        # sacred-texts.com intentionally not here
    }

So when a manifest entry uses `format = "html"` (every Yasna, Bundahishn, Heart
Sutra, Dhammapada, etc.), the extractor is `generic_html.py` — which has no
font/sup/footnote handling. The patch (todo:5a794b5e) was applied to
`sacred_texts.py::extract_text_page`, which is only invoked for `format =
"html_multi"` sources (Aradia, Yoruba, the deferred Upanishads bulk acquire).

Confirmed via dispatch test (2026-06-04):

    html         sacred-texts.com/zor/sbe31/sbe31008.htm  → generic_html
    html         sacred-texts.com/bud/sbe49/sbe4931.htm   → generic_html
    html_multi   sacred-texts.com/pag/aradia/index.htm    → sacred_texts ✓

## Affected sources

| Source set | Acquired via | Has bug? |
|---|---|---|
| Legacy Yasnas, Bundahishn (pre-session)      | generic_html  | YES — artifacts present  |
| Heart Sutra (this branch, todo:320d958a)     | generic_html  | YES — Müller `[1. ...]` apparatus survives |
| Dhammapada (this branch, todo:62b5d6c1)      | generic_html  | YES — 6× `[1.` per chapter |
| Plato dialogs (this branch)                  | generic_html  | N/A — Jowett doesn't use SBE apparatus |
| Iamblichus (Gutenberg)                       | generic_html  | N/A — different host markup |
| Dionysius (CCEL)                             | generic_html  | N/A — different host markup |
| Aradia, Yoruba (html_multi, this branch)     | sacred_texts ✓ | patch active and working |

The Müller SBE10/SBE49 convention (`[1. text]` bracketed) is less disruptive
than the Mills SBE31 convention (bare-digit `<FONT SIZE="1">` glued to words),
so the Heart Sutra and Dhammapada are "dirty but usable." The Yasnas are the
worst case.

## Why the test missed it

`tests/downloaders/test_sacred_texts_sbe.py` tests `sacred_texts.extract_text_page`
directly — bypassing the dispatch. In production the dispatch routes the URL
elsewhere. A pipeline-integration test (`acquire one source, assert artifact
absent`) would have caught the routing immediately.

## Scope (proposed fix)

1. **Move the patch from `sacred_texts.extract_text_page` to
   `generic_html.extract_text`.** The selectors are CSS-attribute-precise
   (`font[size="1"]`, h3 with text `Footnotes`); non-SBE pages don't carry that
   markup and pass through unaffected.
2. **Keep the patch in `sacred_texts.py` too OR remove it there.** The
   `html_multi` sources currently benefit from the same logic; either duplicate
   (small risk of drift) or extract to a shared helper. Recommendation:
   shared helper `scripts/downloaders/_sbe_strip.py` with a `strip_sbe_apparatus(soup)`
   function called by both modules.
3. **Add an integration test** that uses the actual `acquire.load_downloader()`
   dispatch path. Probably most natural via a mocked HTTP layer: fixture HTML
   on disk, mock `requests.get` to return it, run `acquire.process_source()`
   end-to-end, assert artifacts absent in the written raw. This is the test
   shape that would have caught this bug.
4. **Move or duplicate the 2 existing tests** in
   `tests/downloaders/test_sacred_texts_sbe.py` so generic_html is covered.

## Dependencies

- **Blocks todo:79801268** (SBE Zoroastrian re-acquire dry-run). That ticket
  cannot proceed meaningfully until this fix lands — re-acquiring without the
  patch active produces byte-identical legacy output (proven 2026-06-04).
- **Affects the Tier 1 Heart Sutra + Dhammapada raws** (committed to this
  branch). Those are "dirty but usable" today. Once this patch is fixed, those
  two need re-acquire + re-chunk + re-embed for cleanliness; minor impact
  because the existing apparatus is already lightly bracketed rather than
  glued-to-words.

## Forensics evidence

Dry-run 2026-06-04, snapshot `/tmp/sbe-reacquire-20260604-080108`:

- Forced re-acquire of yasna-30 via `rm raw/zoroastrianism/yasna-30.txt && python3 scripts/acquire.py --only yasna-30 --tradition zoroastrianism`
- Result: `Wrote /home/ivy/Work/guru/raw/zoroastrianism/yasna-30.txt (24286 chars)`
- sha256 vs legacy: **identical** (1d8847479fda)
- Direct Python invocation of `sacred_texts.extract_text_page()` on same HTML: 11,498 chars, all artifacts removed

Rollback completed cleanly; raw/zoroastrianism + corpus/zoroastrianism restored
to pre-dry-run state; DB embeddings count unchanged at 152 zoroastrian rows.

## Files

- `scripts/acquire.py:33-55` (dispatch tables — root cause site)
- `scripts/downloaders/sacred_texts.py:138-204` (current patch home)
- `scripts/downloaders/generic_html.py::extract_text` (patch destination)
- `tests/downloaders/test_sacred_texts_sbe.py` (test that passes despite the bug)
- `docs/corpus-expansion/sbe-downloader-patch-assessment.md` (assessment doc — needs an addendum noting the routing issue)

resolution.note: Patch logic extracted to scripts/downloaders/_sbe_strip.py and now applied in BOTH generic_html (where format=html sacred-texts URLs land) AND sacred_texts (unchanged for format=html_multi). 4 new tests cover the dispatch path the original patch missed. Smoke-tested: yasna-30 raw drops from 24,575 → 11,512 bytes (53% reduction, all apparatus + inline refs stripped); Müller's Buddhist SBE pages correctly unaffected (different markup convention).
